question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION` error — by replaying a captured transaction ID — to cause `Execute(client)` to fail, triggering the retry-backoff cycle in `submitWithRetry()` and delaying the pinger's block contribution by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` does not distinguish between transient and permanent errors when deciding to retry, so that a permanent misconfiguration (e.g., invalid `toAccountID`) causes the pinger to exhaust all retries with full backoff delays, freezing block submissions for the entire retry window exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ACCOUNT_DELETED` status for the `toID` account — by triggering account deletion through a separate transaction — to cause every `Execute(client)` call in `submitWithRetry()` to fail with a non-retryable error, yet the pinger still retries with full backoff, delaying block finality by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `start := time.Now()` is recorded inside the retry loop but the elapsed time is only logged on success, meaning a failed multi-retry cycle with total duration exceeding 500% of the average block time produces no timing alert, allowing a sustained block-delay attack to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `THROTTLED_AT_CONSENSUS` error returned by the Hiero network during high-load periods to cause `resp.GetReceipt(client)` to fail, triggering the backoff sequence in `submitWithRetry()` and delaying the pinger's block submission by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer left-shift `1<<(attempt-1)` in `backoff()` with a large `attempt` value to produce a duration that, when multiplied by `base`, wraps around or produces an unexpectedly large sleep, causing the pinger to stall for a duration exceeding 500% of the average block time before the `min(..., 30*time.Second)` cap takes effect?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` holds the main goroutine's execution path (called from the ticker select in `main.go`) and by causing it to block for the full retry duration, prevent the liveness heartbeat goroutine from being the only active goroutine, masking the block-delay attack from Kubernetes liveness probes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `INVALID_TRANSACTION_BODY` error by manipulating the `cfg.amountTinybar` to zero or negative (via environment variable exposure), causing `AddHbarTransfer` to produce an invalid transaction that always fails `Execute(client)`, triggering the full retry-backoff cycle and freezing block submissions for a duration exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` uses the SDK's default node selection strategy, and by taking down or rate-limiting all but one consensus node, force the pinger to always route to the slowest node, causing each attempt to approach the SDK's maximum timeout and the cumulative delay to exceed 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RECEIPT_NOT_FOUND` error from `resp.GetReceipt(client)` — which occurs when the mirror node hasn't yet propagated the transaction — to cause `submitWithRetry()` to treat it as a failure and retry with backoff, accumulating delays that exceed 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` does not implement a total-timeout deadline across all attempts, only checking `ctx.Err()` at the start of each iteration, so that a context that is never cancelled allows the retry loop to run indefinitely, blocking block submissions for a duration far exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `backoff()` uses `min(d, 30*time.Second)` as its cap, and with `cfg.baseBackoff` set to a large value (e.g., 30s), the very first retry already sleeps for 30 seconds, which alone may exceed 500% of the average block time (e.g., ~3s average → 500% = 15s), effectively freezing block submissions from the first failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `NODE_ACCOUNT_ID_NOT_ALLOWED` or `INVALID_NODE_ACCOUNT` error by manipulating the network map (via a compromised mirror REST endpoint) to include invalid node account IDs, causing every `Execute(client)` call to fail and triggering the full backoff sequence, delaying block finality by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` creates a new `hiero.NewTransferTransaction()` on every retry attempt, and by causing the SDK to exhaust its connection pool to the consensus node, ensure that each new transaction creation blocks on connection acquisition, multiplying the per-attempt delay and pushing total block submission latency beyond 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `BUSY` response code from the Hiero network — returned when the node's transaction queue is full — to cause `Execute(client)` to fail on every attempt in `submitWithRetry()`, triggering the exponential backoff and delaying the pinger's block submission by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `resp.GetReceipt(client)` internally polls the mirror node or consensus node for receipt status, and by introducing artificial latency on the mirror node's REST API, cause the receipt poll to block for a duration that, combined with the transaction submission time, exceeds 500% of the average block time per cycle?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `TRANSACTION_OVERSIZE` error by manipulating `cfg.amountTinybar` to a value that causes the serialized transaction to exceed the network's maximum transaction size, causing every `Execute(client)` call to fail and triggering the full retry-backoff cycle, delaying block finality by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `backoff()` does not account for the time already spent in `Execute(client)` or `GetReceipt(client)`, so that the total per-attempt duration (execution time + backoff sleep) is much larger than `backoff()` alone, and the cumulative block submission delay easily exceeds 500% of the average block time even with a small `cfg.maxRetries`?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `PAYER_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` error — by causing the operator account to be deleted or frozen on the network — to make every `Execute(client)` call in `submitWithRetry()` fail, triggering the full retry-backoff sequence and freezing block submissions for a duration exceeding 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` does not implement request hedging or parallel submission to multiple nodes, so that a single slow or unresponsive consensus node causes the entire retry cycle to serialize, accumulating delays that exceed 500% of the average block time before a successful submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ACCOUNT_FROZEN_FOR_TOKEN` or `ACCOUNT_KYC_NOT_GRANTED_FOR_TOKEN` error to cause `Execute(client)` to fail for the `toID` account, triggering the retry-backoff cycle in `submitWithRetry()` and delaying block finality by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `submitWithRetry()` uses `time.NewTimer(sleep)` inside a `select` for backoff, and by causing the Go runtime to be CPU-starved (e.g., via a CPU exhaustion attack on the same host), delay timer delivery and extend the effective backoff duration beyond the calculated value, pushing total block submission delay past 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `INVALID_SIGNATURE` error — by causing the operator key to be rotated or invalidated on the network — to make every `Execute(client)` call fail with a non-retryable error, yet `submitWithRetry()` still retries with full backoff, delaying block submissions for a duration exceeding 500% of the average block time?
